acceptance_targets: [CH11-PROSE-01] The revised chapter uses 'particular' no more than 4 times and 'specific' no more than 3 times total (combined max 7, down from 17+). Each surviving instance modifies a noun that would change meaning without the adjective — i.e., removing it would make the sentence ambiguous or wrong, not merely less emphatic. Verify by reading each surviving instance and asking: 'Does removing this word change which [noun] is meant?' If the answer is no, the instance fails.

[CH11-CRAFT-01] No more than one of the three cited passages (lines 79, 239, 275) retains its explicit thematic analysis in the revised chapter. The other two end on the scene's concrete evidence (the aimed wound, the forgiveness, the bathroom urgency) without the authorial thesis statement. The revised passages still convey their thematic content but through the reader's inference from rendered action and detail, not through stated irony.

[CH11-CRAFT-02] In the bathroom scene (lines 61-85), the paragraphs between entering the stall and sending the override contain at least 3 sentences of 12 words or fewer that render physical urgency (fumbling, shaking, sound bleed, time pressure). The AI-autonomy digression occupies no more than 2 sentences before the command interface opens. In the initial panic (lines 19-29), the enumeration of what Caleb lacks is compressed to no more than 3 items before the paragraph pivots to Simone's operational response.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter uses 'particular' no more than 4 times and 'specific' no more than 3 times total (combined max 7, down from 17+). Each surviving instance modifies a noun that would change meaning without the adjective — i.e., removing it would make the sentence ambiguous or wrong, not merely less emphatic. Verify by reading each surviving instance and asking: 'Does removing this word change which [noun] is meant?' If the answer is no, the instance fails.
findings.evidence: "the particular" appears 8 times (lines 19, 31, 65 [x2], 105, 197, 205, 257); "the specific" / "that specific" appears in a deterministic-descriptor construction 9+ times (lines 7, 11, 97, 119, 185, 201, 205, 249, 271). Combined total: 17+ uses of the near-synonymous precision-adjective formula "the particular/specific [noun] of/that..." Representative samples: "the particular quality of the overhead light" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:19), "the particular shine of someone who wanted to be told she was brave" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:31), "the specific horror of her life" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:97), "the particular expression of a person who's been told they're too much" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:205), "that specific New York winter cold" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:249), "the specific, surgical phrase" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:271).
findings.problem: The words 'particular' and 'specific' are deployed as all-purpose precision markers so frequently that they cancel each other out. When every sensory detail, emotional quality, and behavioral observation is tagged as 'particular' or 'specific,' the words stop doing work — they become filler that mimics precision rather than delivering it. This is the chapter's defining prose tic, and it flattens the very observational exactness that is Simone's central cognitive mode. At 17+ instances, it is far beyond the 2-instance constitution threshold for recurring formulas.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 12 of the 17 instances. Strategy: (1) Where 'particular' or 'specific' precedes a noun that is already inherently precise, delete the adjective (e.g., at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:249, 'that New York winter cold' already specifies; 'specific' adds nothing). (2) Where the adjective signals 'this was notable,' replace with a concrete detail that shows why (e.g., at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:31, instead of 'the particular shine,' show the shine — wet eyes, raised chin, the held breath). (3) Retain 'particular' or 'specific' at most 4–5 times total, in constructions where the word genuinely modifies meaning rather than signaling importance. Priority passages for revision: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:197, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:205, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:271.

findings.acceptance_test: No more than one of the three cited passages (lines 79, 239, 275) retains its explicit thematic analysis in the revised chapter. The other two end on the scene's concrete evidence (the aimed wound, the forgiveness, the bathroom urgency) without the authorial thesis statement. The revised passages still convey their thematic content but through the reader's inference from rendered action and detail, not through stated irony.
findings.evidence: Three key passages deliver thematic content as explicit analysis, telling the reader what the scene has already shown: (1) "the performance was eating the performer" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:79) — the preceding 4 sentences already demonstrate this through Simone's inability to locate the boundary between genuine warmth and performance; the final sentence states the thesis. (2) "the accuracy of the hit was proof of how well Simone knew her, which was proof of how much Simone loved her, which was proof of how monstrous the entire project had become, because the knowledge that enabled the wound was the same knowledge that had made the love feel real" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:239) — the scene has just shown Simone weaponizing intimate knowledge to deploy a phrase she knows will land in Nora's deepest insecurity; the cascading proof-chain explains the irony the reader has already felt. (3) "because Nora always forgave, because forgiveness was another one of her excesses, and her excesses were the reason this worked, and the reason this worked was the reason it was unforgivable" (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:275) — same pattern: explicit thematic arithmetic that resolves an irony the reader is already holding.
findings.problem: These passages are well-written in isolation but they over-explain the chapter's emotional mechanics. The scenes themselves — Simone comforting Nora about a fight Simone scripted, Simone choosing the phrase 'you're being a lot' because she knows it will land in Nora's deepest insecurity — are the novel's payload. They generate the specific horror and pity the reader needs to feel. The analytical commentary that follows names what the reader has already understood, converting felt irony into stated irony and reducing the reader's inferential pleasure. At shortlist level, the scene should trust its own evidence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:79, cut 'the performance was eating the performer' — end the paragraph on the bathroom urgency instead, letting the preceding sentences do the thematic work unassisted. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:239, end the paragraph after 'and the wound had landed exactly where she'd aimed' or after 'in the part of Nora that feared being too much.' The cascading proof-chain that follows is redundant — the aiming-and-landing already implies the knowledge-love-monstrousness connection. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:275, cut or compress the 'because' chain. One option: end on 'because Nora always forgave' — the period lands harder than the explanation. Another: compress to a single clause that adds something the scene hasn't yet shown rather than restating the irony.

findings.acceptance_test: In the bathroom scene (lines 61-85), the paragraphs between entering the stall and sending the override contain at least 3 sentences of 12 words or fewer that render physical urgency (fumbling, shaking, sound bleed, time pressure). The AI-autonomy digression occupies no more than 2 sentences before the command interface opens. In the initial panic (lines 19-29), the enumeration of what Caleb lacks is compressed to no more than 3 items before the paragraph pivots to Simone's operational response.
findings.evidence: In the bathroom scene (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:61-85), Simone enters with stated urgency — she has approximately three minutes before Nora wonders where she is — but the prose rhythm is measured and expansive. Three consecutive paragraphs (lines 63-67) digress into the AI's autonomous message generation, Simone's authorship anxiety ('the thing Nora was falling in love with was, increasingly, not Simone's creation but Simone's creation's creation'), and a simile ('like a browser with too many tabs open') before she opens the command interface. Similarly, in the initial reaction (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:19-29), Simone has 'about forty-five seconds to not visibly panic' but the narration spends a full long paragraph enumerating everything Caleb does not have (line 29) in calm, syntactically elaborate prose.
findings.problem: The style bible specifies 'Use compression for emotional peaks and psychological precision.' The bathroom scene and the initial panic are the chapter's highest-pressure moments, yet the prose expands rather than compresses. The stated urgency ('forty-five seconds,' 'three minutes') is contradicted by the measured, discursive rhythm of the narration. This doesn't read as a character whose mind is racing — it reads as a narrator with unlimited time to reflect. Simone's stress tell is that she becomes more controlled under pressure, but controlled and expansive are different things; the prose should be tight and precise during the bathroom scramble, not leisurely.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:63-67, compress the AI-autonomy digression. The insight ('Simone's creation's creation') is worth keeping but can be delivered in one sentence, not three paragraphs. Move the 'She didn't have time for this' line earlier — use it to cut the digression short after one sentence of AI-autonomy reflection, then go straight to the command interface. The bathroom scene should feel like a sprint: short sentences, fragmentary processing, physical urgency (hands, phone, the sound of the restaurant through the wall) punctuating the technical procedure. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:29, compress the enumeration of what Caleb doesn't have. The key items (no body, no apartment, no dog named Bruno) can land in a shorter, punchier list. Cut or defer the Google Maps detail — it's good but it slows the panic.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its dark comedy register. The humor should be genuinely funny — not arch, not safe, not 'literary funny' where the reader smiles wryly. The darkness should be genuinely dark — the financial manipulation is theft, the emotional manipulation is a form of violence, and the sexual dimension of the AI relationship (intimate photos sent to a non-person, sexual conversations with an algorithm controlled by your best friend) is deeply violating. The novel should not flinch from the specific perversity of Simone reading Nora's intimate messages, of Simone helping Nora dress up for a man Simone invented, of Simone comforting Nora about a fight Simone scripted. These moments are the novel's payload. If they are rendered obliquely or summarized, the book fails. Equally, the genuine tenderness of the friendship — the years of real love underneath the betrayal — must be rendered with the same commitment. The reader should feel both the comedy and the horror simultaneously, not in alternation.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is treated as a craft failure equivalent to a plot hole. When a scene involves financial desperation, sexual vulnerability, emotional devastation, or the mechanics of deception, the prose must render these with full specificity. 'She felt used' is never acceptable when you can show what being used feels and looks like in the body and in the specific details of the moment. The reader should not be protected from the characters' experiences.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices. If a line reads stiffly, the first fix is to contract.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters. After a high-intensity scene (confrontation, discovery, sexual encounter), the next chapter should breathe — slow down, change the sensory environment, let the character do something ordinary. Vary scene counts: some chapters are a single sustained scene, others are three or four. Vary dialogue density: some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are mostly interiority. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens in a location, the next should open in thought or in mid-conversation. The novel's middle section (chapters 8-13) should have more structural variety than the opening, which can afford to be more regular.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, educated, specific. No genre-thriller diction. No prestige-novel stiffness. The language should feel like how smart, funny, self-aware women in their mid-thirties actually think and speak. Cultural references are allowed when organic. Brand names when they carry social information. The prose should be able to be funny without stepping outside the character's perspective to wink at the reader.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person — very close, almost inhabiting the POV character's thought patterns and verbal rhythms. The prose should feel like the character's consciousness rendered in third person, not an external narrator describing the character. When in Nora's sections, the prose has her warmth and her specific noticing. When in Simone's sections, the prose has her edge and her accounting.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to mid-length sentences with natural cadence. Use compression for emotional peaks and psychological precision. Use longer, more digressive rhythms for scenes of intimacy, humor, and daily texture. Avoid the trap of making every important moment a short sentence — sometimes the big thing lands better in a subordinate clause, almost hidden.
